statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: The input consists of two lines. The first line contains a single integer n, indicating the number of elements in the queue. The second line contains n space-separated integers representing the elements in the queue.
statement.outputFormat: Display the dequeued elements in the order they are removed from the queue.
statement.notes: Remove elements from the front of the queue and display them, followed by a newline at the end.

examples.explanation: Dequeue elements in the order they are added to the queue.

hints.text: Make sure to dequeue elements from the front, not the rear, of the queue.

editorial.title: Correcting the Dequeue Method
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to implement a Queue data structure and dequeue elements from the front.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves manually manipulating the queue data structure to dequeue elements in the correct order.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution involves implementing the Queue class with correct dequeue logic.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof involves demonstrating the correctness of the dequeue method.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run involves demonstrating the correct dequeue operation.
commonMistakes: Dequeueing from the rear of the queue
